lines: Find a position where your body feels grounded. [long pause] Feet on the floor if you're sitting. [long pause] Or lying down with your arms at your sides. [long pause] Whatever lets you feel stable.
Close your eyes. [long pause] Or soften your gaze. [long pause] Take a slow breath in... [long pause] [long pause] and let it go.
Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] and out.

Now bring your attention to your feet. [long pause] Feel them against the floor, or the bed, or whatever's beneath them. [long pause] Feel the solidity of that contact. [long pause] You are here. [long pause] You are held up by something beneath you. [long pause] Start there.

Move your attention up through your legs. [long pause] Your hips. [long pause] Your belly. [long pause] Notice what's tight. [long pause] Notice what's soft. [long pause] You don't have to change anything. [long pause] Just arrive in your body.
Your chest. [long pause] Your shoulders. [long pause] Your neck. [long pause] Your face. [long pause] Soften your forehead. [long pause] Let your jaw drop just slightly.
There is a voice inside you that tells you who you are. [long pause] Sometimes it's kind. [long pause] Often... [long pause] [long pause] it's not.
"You're not enough. [long pause] You're too much. [long pause] You should be further along. [long pause] Everyone else has it together. [long pause] You're faking it. [long pause] You're failing."

You know this voice. [long pause] It's been with you a long time. [long pause] It speaks with authority, as if it has the final word on your identity. [long pause] And somewhere along the way, you started believing it. [long pause] Not because it was true — but because it was relentless.
I want you to listen to it right now. [long pause] Not to obey it. [long pause] To observe it. [long pause] Like a sound in the room that you've stopped noticing because it's always been there.
What does your inner critic say most often? [long pause] What's the recurring accusation? [long pause] The sentence it loops back to again and again?
Name it. [long pause] Quietly, inwardly. [long pause] "My inner critic says I am... [long pause] [long pause] " and let it finish.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] whose voice is that, really? [long pause] A parent? [long pause] A teacher? [long pause] A coach? [long pause] A past version of yourself? [long pause] Culture? [long pause] The internet? [long pause] It has a source. [long pause] And that source is not God.

You may have heard it so many times that it sounds like your own voice. [long pause] But it was planted. [long pause] It came from somewhere. [long pause] And it has been watered by repetition, not by truth.

Here's what I want you to do. [long pause] Don't argue with it. [long pause] Don't try to replace it with something positive. [long pause] Not yet. [long pause] For now, just... [long pause] [long pause] be with it. [long pause] Sit in the same room with it. [long pause] Feel what it does to your body.
Where does that accusation land? [long pause] In your chest? [long pause] Your stomach? [long pause] Your throat? [long pause] Does it make you feel small? [long pause] Heavy? [long pause] Hollow?

Stay there. [long pause] I know it's uncomfortable. [long pause] Most of the time we rush past this part. [long pause] We jump to the reassurance, the Bible verse, the "but God says... [long pause] [long pause] " And that's not wrong. [long pause] But if we skip over the pain, the truth doesn't have anywhere to land.
So stay. [long pause] Breathe. [long pause] Let the ache be there.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] bring this to God. [long pause] Not as a prayer you've rehearsed. [long pause] Not as a request to fix it. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] show it to him. [long pause] The way a child brings a broken toy to a parent. [long pause] Not with an explanation. [long pause] Just with open hands.
"This is what I carry. [long pause] This is what the voice says. [long pause] This is how it makes me feel."

And listen. [long pause] Not for a thundering answer. [long pause] Not for a corrective verse. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] listen. [long pause] What is the quality of God's attention toward you right now? [long pause] Is it impatient? [long pause] Disappointed? [long pause] Rushed?
Paul writes to the Corinthians: "Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come. [long pause] The old has gone, the new is here." Second Corinthians five, seventeen.

New creation. [long pause] Not a repaired version of the old. [long pause] Not the same broken thing with a fresh coat of paint. [long pause] Something new. [long pause] Something that didn't exist before.

The inner critic speaks from the old creation. [long pause] It references the old data. [long pause] The old failures, the old labels, the old metrics. [long pause] It doesn't know about the new thing. [long pause] It can't see it, because it's looking in the wrong direction.

And to the Galatians, Paul writes something even more startling: "I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. [long pause] The life I now live in the body, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me." Galatians two, twenty.

Your deepest identity is not your performance. [long pause] Not your appearance. [long pause] Not your productivity. [long pause] Not your worst moment or your best. [long pause] It is this: Christ lives in you. [long pause] The Spirit of the living God has made a home in your body. [long pause] You are indwelt. [long pause] Inhabited. [long pause] Not by an idea of love — by a person.

This is not denial. [long pause] The struggles are real. [long pause] The pain is real. [long pause] The critic will keep talking — maybe for the rest of your life. [long pause] But the identity beneath the struggle is secure. [long pause] It does not depend on your ability to silence the critic. [long pause] It does not depend on your performance today or tomorrow. [long pause] It was settled before you did anything to earn it or lose it.

Let's ground that in your body. [long pause] Feel your feet again. [long pause] The contact with the floor. [long pause] Feel the chair or the bed beneath you. [long pause] The solidity. [long pause] The support.

Feel your hands. [long pause] Open them if they're closed. [long pause] Turn your palms upward if you can. [long pause] This is the posture of receiving. [long pause] Not earning. [long pause] Not grabbing. [long pause] Receiving.
You are not what happened to you. [long pause] You are not what you've done. [long pause] You are not the verdict of the loudest voice in the room.

You are who God says you are. [long pause] And what God says... [long pause] [long pause] is spoken not as correction, but as recognition. [long pause] The way a father looks at a child and says, "That's my kid."

Let's borrow from the contemplative tradition and try what we might call a prayer of identity. [long pause] It's simple. [long pause] You hold your true name before God — not the name the critic gave you, but the name God knows you by.
So try this. [long pause] Inwardly, say: "I am yours."
One more time. [long pause] Not louder. [long pause] Quieter. [long pause] Let it sink below thought. [long pause] "I am yours."
And now, from that place... [long pause] [long pause] let me pray.

Father... [long pause] [long pause] we bring you the voices that have told us who we are. [long pause] The critical ones. [long pause] The shaming ones. [long pause] The ones we've memorized. [long pause] We don't ask you to shout over them. [long pause] We ask you to speak beneath them. [long pause] In the quiet place under the noise.

Remind us. [long pause] Not once but daily. [long pause] That our identity is not up for debate. [long pause] That we are new creations. [long pause] That Christ lives in us — not as a metaphor but as a reality. [long pause] That we are yours before we are anything else.
And when the old voice speaks... [long pause] [long pause] give us the grace to hear it, name it, and let it pass. [long pause] Not because it has no power — but because you have more.
We pray this in the name of Jesus, who looked at broken, failing, doubting people and called them his own.
Stay here for a moment. [long pause] Feel your body. [long pause] Feel the ground beneath you. [long pause] Feel the air moving in and out of your lungs.
When you're ready... [long pause] [long pause] begin to move gently. [long pause] Your fingers. [long pause] Your toes. [long pause] A slow stretch if your body wants one.
You are a new creation. [long pause] Not because you feel like one. [long pause] Because you are one.
Carry that today. [long pause] Lightly. [long pause] The way you'd carry something precious that doesn't belong to you — because it was given.
